reason: The item cleanly tests a single engineering judgment axis: how simultaneous shifts in LFL, LOC, and explosion severity metrics change ignition risk and inerting/venting design conservatism. Correct options are tightly tied to the stated empirical trends, while incorrect options hinge on classic over-extrapolations (proportional scaling; scope transfer), creating meaningful expert discrimination without requiring outside facts. Minor weakness: one correct implication (vent panel sizing) relies on an implicit link from (dp/dt)max to vent design conservatism, which most experts accept but is not explicitly stated in the stem.

The item cleanly targets phase-dependent inference: it forces solvers to reject an overgeneral SOC→particulate claim by naming the explosion-phase reversal and its limiting mechanism (violence-driven dispersion/dilution affecting measured soot number). Boundary conditions are fairly tight (specific chemistries, SOC points, and event phases), but as a short-answer item it has slightly softer decoy control and may allow partially correct responses that gesture at “phase dependence” without specifying the >50% SOC explosion-phase reversal mechanism.
